headlines.text: US F-15 shot down over Iran; search-and-rescue operation underway in central Iran
Trump fires AG Pam Bondi; Todd Blanche named acting attorney general
Hegseth ousts Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George and two other senior generals
Iran strikes desalination plants in Kuwait, UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Bahrain
March jobs report: US added 178,000 jobs, beating all forecasts
Gas prices hit $4.09 nationally -- first time above $4 in four years
Tiger Woods bodycam video released; judge grants permission for overseas treatment
Super Mario Galaxy Movie breaks records with $68M global opening day
UK holds talks with 40 nations on Strait of Hormuz; US sits out
ActBlue legal memos published by NYT allege platform may have committed federal crimes

breaking.text: US F-15 fighter jet shot down over Iran; search and rescue underway for crew

stories.hed: US F-15 Downed Over Iran as Trump Threatens to Destroy 'What's Left'
stories.dek: A US F-15 went down over central Iran today -- whether shot down or suffering a bad day mechanically remains unclear -- while Trump posted that the US 'hasn't even started destroying what's left in Iran.' Iran helpfully offered a reward for locals who find the crew, which is one way to do search and rescue.

stories.hed: UK Rallies 40 Nations on Hormuz; US Declines to Attend Its Own War's Cleanup
stories.dek: Britain hosted 40 countries to discuss reopening the Strait of Hormuz, through which roughly 20 percent of global oil and LNG flows. The US, which started the war, did not participate, apparently busy tweeting about making a fortune from the oil instead.

stories.hed: Bondi Out, Blanche In: Trump Reshuffles DOJ as Epstein Files Loom
stories.dek: Trump ousted Attorney General Pam Bondi and installed former personal attorney Todd Blanche as acting AG, with the Epstein files casting a long shadow over the transition. The New York Times simultaneously published confidential ActBlue legal memos suggesting Democrats' biggest fundraising platform may have committed federal crimes.

stories.hed: Hegseth Fires Army's Top General and Two Others Mid-War
stories.dek: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth abruptly dismissed Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George along with two other senior officers, with no official explanation given. Nothing says wartime confidence like firing your top general on Day 35 of an active conflict.

stories.hed: Trump Wants to 'Make a Fortune' Off Iran's Oil While the World Pays $4+ at the Pump
stories.dek: Trump posted this morning that the US could 'easily OPEN THE HORMUZ STRAIT, TAKE THE OIL, & MAKE A FORTUNE' -- a statement that landed differently for the 330 million Americans currently paying over $4 a gallon. Gas is up more than $1 since February 26, a 33 percent spike that economists are calling the sharpest one-month increase in recent history.

markets.context: US markets are closed for Good Friday; futures slipped 0.3% after a stronger-than-expected March jobs report reduced Fed cut bets, while oil surged to $111 on the downed F-15 and Trump's escalation threats.

gas.note: Filling up a 15-gallon tank now costs $19 more than it did before the war. The Strait of Hormuz is closed; your wallet is too.

popCulture.hed: Super Mario Galaxy Movie Blasts Off With $68M Global Opening Day, Crushing Its Predecessor
popCulture.dek: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie launched to a record $34.5M domestic opening day on April 1 -- the biggest of 2026 -- and $68.4M worldwide, edging out the original Super Mario Bros. Movie. Critics gave it 43% on Rotten Tomatoes; audiences gave it 91%, which tells you everything about who buys popcorn.

genZ.definition: A TikTok meme format featuring a skeleton frantically banging a shield, used to represent chaotic, overwhelming, or absurd situations that you just have to endure. It exploded in early 2026 as one of the first major meme formats of the year, with creators applying it to everything from work stress to global news.
genZ.usage: Me watching Trump threaten to destroy Iran's power plants while simultaneously tweeting about making a fortune off their oil: [skeleton banging shield at full speed].

tldr: On Good Friday 2026, a US F-15 went down over Iran on Day 35 of the war, Trump fired his attorney general and the Army's top general before breakfast, and gas hit $4.09 nationally. The Super Mario Galaxy Movie opened to a record $68M worldwide, providing the only uncomplicated joy of the week.
